Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the peak of the movement. Avoid using momentum to pull the band.
Pose.Name: Resistance Band Row
Pose.Equipment: Resistance Band, Anchor Point
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Shoulder impingement, elbow tendinitis, lower back injury

steps.step: Anchor a resistance band at chest height. Hold one end in each hand, step back to create tension. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent.
Exhale as you pull the band toward your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Keep your elbows close to your body.
Pause for 1 second at the peak, feeling your upper back contract.
Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps.
Keep your shoulders down and back—don't let them shrug up toward your ears. Focus on squeezing your shoulder blades, not pulling with your arms.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using momentum to pull the band.
Not fully extending arms at the end of each rep.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and chest up. Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the peak. Avoid jerking the weight. Control the return.
Pose.Name: Seated Cable Row (Light)
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with V-Grip Handle
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Rhomboids, Trapezius, Latissimus Dorsi, Rear Deltoids, Biceps
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Lower back injury, shoulder impingement, elbow tendinitis

steps.step: Sit at the cable row machine with your feet braced. Grip the V-handle, keep your back straight, chest up, and core braced.
Exhale as you pull the handle toward your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Keep your elbows close to your body.
Pause for 1 second at the peak, feeling your upper back contract.
Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position, feeling a stretch in your upper back. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps.
Do not lean back excessively during the row. Keep your torso mostly upright—only a slight lean is natural.

common_mistakes.mistake: Leaning too far back to use momentum.
Pulling with the arms instead of the back.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Hinge at your hips. Avoid rounding your lower back. Squeeze your shoulder blades at the top of the row.
Pose.Name: Dumbbell Bent-Over Row
Pose.Equipment: Light Dumbbells, Bench (Optional for support)
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Rhomboids, Trapezius, Latissimus Dorsi, Rear Deltoids, Biceps, Lower Back (Stabilizer)

steps.step: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent. Hinge at your hips to lean forward until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor. Hold dumbbells with palms facing each other.
Exhale as you pull the dumbbells toward your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Keep your elbows close to your body.
Pause for 1 second at the top, feeling your upper back contract.
Inhale as you lower the dumbbells with control. Perform 2 sets of 10 reps.
If you have trouble maintaining a flat back, place one knee and hand on a bench for support (single-arm supported row).

common_mistakes.mistake: Using momentum to swing the dumbbells.
Pulling with the arms instead of the back.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Squeeze your lats at the bottom of the movement. Avoid using momentum to pull the bar down.
Pose.Name: Lat Pulldown (Light)
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with Wide Bar, Lat Pulldown Station
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Latissimus Dorsi (Primary), Biceps, Rhomboids, Rear Deltoids
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Shoulder impingement, elbow tendinitis, lower back injury

steps.step: Sit at the lat pulldown machine. Grip the bar wider than shoulder-width with palms facing forward. Secure your knees under the pads.
Exhale as you pull the bar down to your upper chest, leading with your elbows. Squeeze your lats at the bottom.
Pause for 1 second at the bottom, feeling your lats contract.
Inhale as you slowly return the bar to the starting position, feeling a stretch in your lats. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps.
Lean back slightly (10-15 degrees) to engage your lats better. Do not use your body weight to pull the bar down—control the movement.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using momentum to swing the bar down.
Pulling behind the neck (dangerous for shoulders).
Letting the bar pull your arms up too quickly.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Squeeze your lats at the bottom of the movement. Avoid using momentum. Control the weight throughout.
Pose.Name: Straight Arm Pulldown
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with Rope or Straight Bar

steps.step: Attach a rope or straight bar to a high pulley. Stand facing the cable, holding the attachment with an overhand grip. Keep a slight bend in your elbows.
Exhale as you pull the attachment down toward your thighs, keeping your arms straight (but not locked). Squeeze your lats at the bottom.
Pause for 1 second at the bottom, feeling your lats contract.
Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position, keeping your arms straight. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps.
This exercise isolates the lats because your arms stay straight. Focus on pulling with your lats, not your shoulders or arms.

common_mistakes.mistake: Bending the elbows during the movement.

Pose.safety: Keep your neck in a neutral position (looking at the floor). Squeeze your glutes and lower back at the top. Do not overextend your spine.
Pose.Name: Superman Hold
Pose.Equipment: Exercise Mat
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Erector Spinae (Lower Back), Glutes, Hamstrings, Core
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Lower back herniation, spinal stenosis, spondylolisthesis

steps.step: Lie face down on a mat with your arms extended overhead and legs straight. Keep your neck in a neutral position (looking at the floor).
Exhale as you simultaneously lift your arms, chest, and legs off the floor. Squeeze your glutes and lower back.
Hold the lifted position for 2-3 seconds, keeping your neck neutral and core engaged.
Inhale as you slowly lower back to the starting position. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps.
Do not lift too high—just a few inches is enough. Focus on squeezing your glutes and lower back, not arching your spine excessively.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Move through a controlled range of motion. Avoid arching your back at the top.
Pose.Name: Back Extension (Bodyweight)
Pose.Equipment: Back Extension Bench or Roman Chair

steps.step: Position yourself on a back extension bench with your hips on the pad and ankles secured. Cross your arms over your chest or place hands behind your head.
Inhale as you slowly lower your upper body toward the floor, keeping your back straight.
Exhale as you lift your upper body back to the starting position, squeezing your lower back and glutes at the top.
Perform 2 sets of 12 reps. Do not hyperextend at the top—stop when your body is in a straight line.
Keep your neck neutral throughout. Do not look up or tuck your chin excessively. The movement comes from your hips, not your spine.

common_mistakes.mistake: Hyperextending the lower back at the top.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Squeeze your traps at the top of the movement. Do not roll your shoulders—lift straight up and down.
Pose.Name: Dumbbell Shrug
Pose.Equipment: Light Dumbbells

steps.step: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides. Keep your back straight and core braced.
Exhale as you lift your shoulders straight up toward your ears as high as possible. Squeeze your traps at the top.
Pause for 1 second at the top, squeezing your traps.
Inhale as you slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position. Perform 2 sets of 15 reps.
Do not roll your shoulders forward or backward—this can strain your neck. Lift straight up and down.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using momentum to jerk the shoulders up.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the peak of the movement. Avoid using momentum.
Pose.Name: Band Pull-Apart
Pose.Equipment: Light Resistance Band
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Trapezius (Middle/Lower), Rhomboids, Rear Deltoids, Rotator Cuff

steps.step: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold a resistance band with both hands shoulder-width apart, arms extended straight in front of you at shoulder height.
Exhale as you pull the band apart by squeezing your shoulder blades together. Keep your arms straight (but not locked).
Pause for 1-2 seconds at the peak, squeezing your upper back.
Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position. Perform 2 sets of 15 reps.
This is a great warm-up and posture exercise. Focus on squeezing your shoulder blades, not just moving your arms.

common_mistakes.mistake: Bending the elbows during the movement.
Not fully returning to the starting position.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Hinge at your hips. Avoid rounding your lower back. Squeeze your shoulder blades at the top of the row.
Pose.Name: Barbell Bent-Over Row
Pose.Equipment: Barbell with Light Plates
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Rhomboids, Trapezius (Middle), Latissimus Dorsi, Rear Deltoids, Biceps, Lower Back

steps.step: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent. Hinge at your hips to lean forward until your torso is at 45-60 degrees. Grip the barbell with hands shoulder-width apart, palms facing you or away.
Exhale as you pull the barbell toward your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Keep your elbows close to your body.
Pause for 1 second at the top, feeling your upper back contract.
Inhale as you lower the barbell with control. Perform 3 sets of 10 reps.
Do not use your lower back to lift the weight. Keep your back straight throughout—imagine a broomstick along your spine.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using momentum to swing the barbell.
Pulling with the arms instead of the back.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and chest up. Squeeze your shoulder blades at the peak. Avoid jerking the weight. Control the return.
Pose.Name: Seated Cable Row
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with V-Grip or Wide Bar
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Rhomboids, Trapezius, Latissimus Dorsi, Rear Deltoids, Biceps

steps.step: Sit at the cable row machine with your feet braced. Grip the handle, keep your back straight, chest up, and core braced.
Exhale as you pull the handle toward your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Keep your elbows close to your body.
Pause for 1-2 seconds at the peak, feeling your upper back contract.
Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
Experiment with different grip attachments—wide grip targets upper back more, close grip targets lats more.

common_mistakes.mistake: Leaning too far back to use momentum.
Pulling with the arms instead of the back.
Letting the weight stack touch between reps.

Pose.safety: Keep your chest pressed against the pad. Squeeze your shoulder blades at the peak. Avoid using momentum. Control the weight throughout.
Pose.Name: Chest-Supported Dumbbell Row
Pose.Equipment: Incline Bench, Dumbbells

steps.step: Set an incline bench to 30-45 degrees. Lie face down on the bench, chest supported, holding dumbbells with palms facing each other.
Exhale as you pull the dumbbells toward your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Keep your elbows close to your body.
Pause for 1-2 seconds at the peak.
Inhale as you lower with control. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
The chest-supported position eliminates lower back strain, allowing you to focus entirely on your upper back muscles.

common_mistakes.mistake: Pulling with arms instead of back.
Not fully extending at the bottom.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Focus on external rotation of the shoulders. Avoid using momentum. Control the weight.
Pose.Name: Face Pull
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with Rope Attachment, High Pulley
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Rear Deltoids, Trapezius (Middle/Lower), Rhomboids, Rotator Cuff (Infraspinatus/Teres Minor)

steps.step: Attach a rope to a high pulley. Stand facing the cable, holding the rope with an overhand grip. Step back to create tension.
Exhale as you pull the rope toward your face, separating the ends. Keep your elbows high (shoulder height).
At the peak, externally rotate your shoulders by pulling the rope ends past your ears. Squeeze your shoulder blades.
Inhale as you return with control. Perform 2 sets of 15 reps.
Face pulls are essential for shoulder health. Use moderate weight and focus on external rotation—this builds balanced, resilient shoulders.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using too much weight, compromising form.
Not externally rotating at the peak.
Letting elbows drop below shoulder height.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Squeeze your lats at the bottom. Avoid using momentum to pull the bar down. Control the return.
Pose.Name: Lat Pulldown
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with Wide Bar, Lat Pulldown Station
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Latissimus Dorsi (Primary), Biceps, Rhomboids, Rear Deltoids

steps.step: Sit at the lat pulldown machine. Grip the bar wider than shoulder-width. Secure your knees under the pads.
Exhale as you pull the bar down to your upper chest, leading with your elbows. Squeeze your lats at the bottom.
Pause for 1-2 seconds at the bottom, feeling your lats contract.
Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
Lean back slightly (10-15 degrees). Imagine pulling your elbows down into your back pockets—this engages the lats effectively.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using body momentum to pull the bar down.
Letting the bar pull your arms up too quickly.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Use a bench for knee support if needed. Squeeze your lat at the peak of the row. Control the movement.
Pose.Name: Single-Arm Dumbbell Row
Pose.Equipment: Dumbbell, Bench
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Latissimus Dorsi (Unilateral), Rhomboids, Trapezius, Biceps, Rear Deltoids

steps.step: Place one knee and hand on a bench, with your other foot on the floor. Hold a dumbbell in your free hand, arm hanging straight down.
Exhale as you pull the dumbbell toward your hip, squeezing your lat. Keep your elbow close to your body.
Pause for 1-2 seconds at the peak, feeling your lat contract.
Inhale as you lower with control. Perform 3 sets of 10 reps per side.
Imagine pulling your elbow toward your back pocket. This cue helps engage the lat rather than just the arm.

common_mistakes.mistake: Rotating the torso during the row.
Using momentum to swing the dumbbell.
Pulling too high (toward chest instead of hip).

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Squeeze your lats at the bottom. Keep your arms straight throughout. Control the weight.
Pose.Name: Straight Arm Pulldown
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with Rope or Straight Bar

steps.step: Attach a rope or straight bar to a high pulley. Stand facing the cable, holding the attachment with an overhand grip. Keep a slight bend in your elbows.
Exhale as you pull the attachment down toward your thighs, keeping your arms straight. Squeeze your lats at the bottom.
Pause for 1-2 seconds at the bottom.
Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps.
This is a lat isolation exercise—don't use heavy weight. Focus on feeling your lats stretch at the top and contract at the bottom.

common_mistakes.mistake: Bending the elbows during the movement.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Move through a controlled range of motion. Avoid hyperextending at the top.
Pose.Name: Back Extension
Pose.Equipment: Back Extension Bench or Roman Chair

steps.step: Position yourself on a back extension bench with your hips on the pad and ankles secured. Cross your arms over your chest or place hands behind your head.
Inhale as you slowly lower your upper body toward the floor, keeping your back straight.
Exhale as you lift your upper body back to the starting position, squeezing your lower back and glutes at the top.
Perform 3 sets of 12 reps. Stop when your body is in a straight line—do not hyperextend.
Keep your neck neutral throughout. The movement should come from your hips, not your spine.

common_mistakes.mistake: Hyperextending the lower back at the top.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Hinge at your hips. Maintain a slight bend in your knees. Do not round your back.
Pose.Name: Romanian Deadlift (Light - Lower Back Focus)
Pose.Equipment: Light Barbell or Dumbbells

steps.step: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding a light barbell or dumbbells in front of your thighs. Keep a slight bend in your knees.
Inhale as you push your hips back, lowering the weight down your shins. Keep your back completely straight.
Lower until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings (typically mid-shin). Do not round your back.
Exhale as you drive your hips forward to return to standing. Perform 3 sets of 10 reps.
Focus on pushing your hips back, not bending your lower back. The movement is a hip hinge, not a squat.

common_mistakes.mistake: Bending the knees too much (turning into a squat).

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Hinge at your hips. Do not round your back. Start with bodyweight only to master form.
Pose.Name: Good Morning (Bodyweight)
Pose.Equipment: None (Bodyweight), Broomstick (Optional for practice)

steps.step: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent. Place a broomstick across your upper back (optional for practice). Keep your back straight.
Inhale as you push your hips back, bending forward at the waist. Keep your back completely straight.
Lower your torso until it is parallel to the floor (or as far as hamstring flexibility allows).
Exhale as you drive your hips forward to return to standing. Squeeze your glutes at the top. Perform 2 sets of 12 reps.
Keep your gaze forward or slightly down. Imagine a line from the top of your head to your tailbone—keep it straight.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using momentum to return to standing.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Squeeze your traps at the top. Lift straight up and down—do not roll your shoulders.
Pose.Name: Barbell Shrug
Pose.Equipment: Barbell with Light to Moderate Plates

steps.step: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip. Keep your back straight and core braced.
Exhale as you lift your shoulders straight up toward your ears as high as possible. Squeeze your traps at the top.
Pause for 1-2 seconds at the top, squeezing your traps.
Inhale as you slowly lower your shoulders back down. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
Do not roll your shoulders. Lift straight up and down. A barbell allows you to use heavier weight than dumbbells.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using momentum to jerk the bar up.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Squeeze your traps at the top. Do not roll your shoulders—lift straight up and down.
Pose.Name: Dumbbell Shrug
Pose.Equipment: Dumbbells

steps.step: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides. Keep your back straight and core braced.
Exhale as you lift your shoulders straight up toward your ears as high as possible. Squeeze your traps at the top.
Pause for 1-2 seconds at the top.
Inhale as you slowly lower your shoulders back down. Perform 3 sets of 15 reps.
Dumbbells allow a slightly different range of motion than barbells. Use both variations for complete trap development.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the peak. Avoid using momentum.
Pose.Name: Band Pull-Apart
Pose.Equipment: Resistance Band (Medium Resistance)

steps.step: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold a resistance band with both hands shoulder-width apart, arms extended straight in front of you at shoulder height.
Exhale as you pull the band apart by squeezing your shoulder blades together. Keep your arms straight.
Pause for 1-2 seconds at the peak, squeezing your upper back.
Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position. Perform 2 sets of 15 reps.
Use this as a warm-up before upper body workouts or as a posture corrective. Focus on squeezing your shoulder blades.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Hinge at your hips. Do not round your lower back. Squeeze your shoulder blades at the top.
Pose.Name: Barbell Bent-Over Row (Heavy)
Pose.Equipment: Barbell with Heavy Plates
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Rhomboids, Trapezius (Middle), Latissimus Dorsi, Rear Deltoids, Biceps, Lower Back

steps.step: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent. Hinge at your hips to lean forward until your torso is at 45 degrees. Grip the barbell slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Exhale as you pull the barbell toward your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Keep your elbows close to your body.
Pause for 1-2 seconds at the peak.
Inhale as you lower with control. Perform 4 sets of 8 reps.
Use a belt for heavy sets. Keep your back straight—if you feel it rounding, the weight is too heavy.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using momentum to swing the barbell.
Pulling with arms instead of back.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Start each rep from a dead stop on the floor. Do not bounce the weight. Squeeze your shoulder blades at the top.
Pose.Name: Pendlay Row
Pose.Equipment: Barbell with Plates
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Rhomboids, Trapezius, Latissimus Dorsi, Rear Deltoids, Biceps, Lower Back

steps.step: Stand over a barbell with feet shoulder-width apart. Hinge at your hips until your torso is parallel to the floor. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Exhale as you explosively pull the barbell toward your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades. Keep your back straight.
Pause briefly at the top.
Inhale as you lower the bar to the floor, coming to a dead stop. Perform 4 sets of 6 reps.
Unlike bent-over rows, Pendlay rows start from a dead stop on the floor each rep. This eliminates momentum and builds explosive strength.

common_mistakes.mistake: Bouncing the bar off the floor.
Not fully stopping the bar on the floor.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and chest up. Squeeze your shoulder blades at the peak. Use a wide grip to target upper back. Control the return.
Pose.Name: Seated Cable Row (Wide Grip)
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with Wide Bar Attachment

steps.step: Sit at the cable row machine with your feet braced. Grip a wide bar attachment. Keep your back straight, chest up, and core braced.
Exhale as you pull the bar toward your upper chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Keep your elbows flared slightly outward.
Pause for 2 seconds at the peak.
Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
A wide grip targets the upper back muscles more than a close grip. Focus on squeezing your shoulder blades, not just pulling with your arms.

common_mistakes.mistake: Pulling with arms instead of back.
Letting the weight stack touch between reps.

Pose.safety: Keep your chest pressed against the pad. Squeeze your shoulder blades at the peak. Avoid using momentum. Control the weight.
Pose.Name: Chest-Supported Row
Pose.Equipment: Chest-Supported Row Machine or Incline Bench with Dumbbells

steps.step: Sit at a chest-supported row machine or lie face down on an incline bench. Grip the handles with palms facing down.
Exhale as you pull the handles toward your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for 2 seconds at the peak, feeling your upper back contract.
Inhale as you lower with control. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
Chest-supported rows remove lower back strain, allowing you to focus entirely on your upper back. Use a slow, controlled tempo.

common_mistakes.mistake: Pulling with arms instead of back.
Not fully extending at the bottom.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Focus on external rotation of the shoulders. Use moderate weight—this is a prehab exercise.
Pose.Name: Face Pull (Heavy - Advanced)
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with Rope Attachment
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Rear Deltoids, Trapezius (Middle/Lower), Rhomboids, Rotator Cuff

steps.step: Attach a rope to a high pulley. Stand facing the cable, holding the rope with an overhand grip. Step back to create tension.
Exhale as you pull the rope toward your face, separating the ends. Keep your elbows high (shoulder height).
At the peak, externally rotate your shoulders by pulling the rope ends past your ears. Squeeze your shoulder blades.
Inhale as you return with control. Perform 3 sets of 15 reps.
Even at advanced levels, don't ego-lift on face pulls. Focus on the external rotation and squeeze—that's where the benefit lies.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using too much weight, compromising form.
Not externally rotating at the peak.
Letting elbows drop below shoulder height.

Pose.safety: Use a controlled motion—no kipping or swinging. Squeeze your lats at the top. Keep your core braced and back straight.
Pose.Name: Weighted Pull-Up
Pose.Equipment: Pull-Up Bar, Weight Belt or Dip Belt, Weight Plates
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Latissimus Dorsi (Primary), Biceps, Rhomboids, Trapezius, Core
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Shoulder impingement, elbow tendinitis, rotator cuff tear

steps.step: Attach a weight plate to a dip belt and secure it around your waist. Grip the pull-up bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulders.
Exhale as you pull yourself up until your chin clears the bar. Squeeze your lats at the top.
Pause for 1 second at the top.
Inhale as you slowly lower with control to full extension. Perform 4 sets of 8 reps.
If you can't complete 8 reps with added weight, drop the weight and master bodyweight pull-ups first. Form is everything.

common_mistakes.mistake: Not fully extending arms at the bottom.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Squeeze your lats at the bottom. Use a close grip to target lower lats. Control the return.
Pose.Name: Lat Pulldown (Close Grip)
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with Close Grip Bar or V-Handle

steps.step: Sit at the lat pulldown machine. Attach a close grip bar or V-handle. Grip with palms facing each other. Secure your knees under the pads.
Exhale as you pull the handle down to your upper chest, leading with your elbows. Squeeze your lats at the bottom.
Pause for 2 seconds at the bottom.
Inhale as you slowly return. Perform 4 sets of 10 reps.
A close grip (palms facing each other) targets the lower lats more effectively than a wide grip. Pull to your upper chest, not behind your neck.

common_mistakes.mistake: Letting the bar pull your arms up too quickly.
Not squeezing lats at the bottom.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Squeeze your lat at the peak of the row. Control the movement—do not use momentum.
Pose.Name: Single-Arm Cable Row
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with Single Handle Attachment

steps.step: Attach a single handle to a low pulley. Sit at the cable row machine, gripping the handle with one hand. Place your other hand on your knee for support.
Exhale as you pull the handle toward your hip, squeezing your lat. Keep your elbow close to your body.
Pause for 2 seconds at the peak.
Inhale as you slowly return. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps per side.
Single-arm rows help correct strength imbalances. Start with your weaker side and match reps on the stronger side.

common_mistakes.mistake: Rotating the torso during the row.
Pulling with arms instead of lats.
Letting the weight stack touch between reps.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Squeeze your lats at the bottom. Keep your arms straight throughout. Control the weight.
Pose.Name: Straight Arm Pulldown
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with Rope or Straight Bar

steps.step: Attach a rope or straight bar to a high pulley. Stand facing the cable, holding the attachment with an overhand grip. Keep a slight bend in your elbows.
Exhale as you pull the attachment down toward your thighs, keeping your arms straight. Squeeze your lats at the bottom.
Pause for 2 seconds at the bottom.
Inhale as you slowly return. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
Use a weight that allows you to feel your lats working. This is an isolation exercise—heavy weight often leads to poor form.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Drive through your heels. Do not round your back at any point. Use a belt for heavy sets.
Pose.Name: Conventional Deadlift
Pose.Equipment: Barbell with Plates, Deadlift Platform, Belt (Optional)
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Erector Spinae (Lower Back), Glutes, Hamstrings, Quadriceps, Traps, Forearms
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Lower back herniation, sciatica, hamstring tear

steps.step: Stand with feet hip-width apart, shins close to the bar. Grip the bar just outside your knees. Keep your back straight, chest up.
Inhale and brace your core. Drive through your heels to stand up, keeping the bar close to your body.
At the top, squeeze your glutes and stand tall. Do not hyperextend your back.
Inhale as you lower the bar with control back to the floor. Perform 4 sets of 6 reps.
The deadlift is a full-body exercise. Keep your lats engaged by imagining you're squeezing oranges in your armpits.

common_mistakes.mistake: Jerking the bar off the floor.
Letting the bar drift away from the body.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Hinge at your hips. Maintain a slight bend in your knees. Do not round your back.
Pose.Name: Romanian Deadlift (Advanced)
Pose.Equipment: Barbell with Plates

steps.step: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell in front of your thighs. Keep a slight bend in your knees and core braced.
Inhale as you push your hips back, lowering the bar down your shins. Keep your back completely straight.
Lower until you feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings (typically mid-shin).
Exhale as you drive your hips forward to return to standing. Perform 4 sets of 8 reps.
The RDL builds hamstring and lower back strength. Use a belt for heavy sets and focus on the stretch.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Hinge at your hips. Do not round your back. Start with light weight to master form.
Pose.Name: Barbell Good Morning
Pose.Equipment: Barbell with Light to Moderate Plates

steps.step: Place a barbell across your upper back (low bar position). Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent.
Inhale as you push your hips back, bending forward at the waist. Keep your back completely straight.
Lower your torso until it is parallel to the floor (or as far as hamstring flexibility allows).
Exhale as you drive your hips forward to return to standing. Perform 3 sets of 10 reps.
Good mornings are unforgiving of poor form. Start with an empty bar and video yourself to check for back rounding.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Move through a controlled range of motion. Do not hyperextend at the top.
Pose.Name: Hyperextension (Weighted)
Pose.Equipment: Back Extension Bench, Weight Plate or Dumbbell

steps.step: Position yourself on a back extension bench with your hips on the pad. Hold a weight plate against your chest.
Inhale as you slowly lower your upper body toward the floor, keeping your back straight.
Exhale as you lift your upper body back to the starting position, squeezing your lower back and glutes.
Perform 3 sets of 12 reps. Do not hyperextend at the top—stop when your body is in a straight line.
Adding weight increases resistance significantly. Start with a small plate (5-10 kg) and progress gradually.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Squeeze your traps at the top. Lift straight up and down—do not roll your shoulders.
Pose.Name: Barbell Shrug (Heavy)
Pose.Equipment: Barbell with Heavy Plates

steps.step: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip. Keep your back straight and core braced.
Exhale as you lift your shoulders straight up toward your ears as high as possible. Squeeze your traps at the top.
Pause for 2 seconds at the top, squeezing your traps.
Inhale as you slowly lower your shoulders back down. Perform 4 sets of 10 reps.
Use lifting straps for heavy shrugs to prevent grip failure before trap fatigue. Do not roll your shoulders.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using momentum to jerk the bar up.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Squeeze your traps at the top. Control the descent (eccentric) phase. Do not roll your shoulders.
Pose.Name: Dumbbell Shrug (Slow Eccentric)
Pose.Equipment: Heavy Dumbbells

steps.step: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a heavy dumbbell in each hand at your sides. Keep your back straight and core braced.
Exhale as you lift your shoulders straight up toward your ears over 1 second. Squeeze your traps at the top.
Pause for 1 second at the top.
Inhale as you slowly lower your shoulders back down over 4 seconds. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
The slow eccentric (lowering) phase builds trap size effectively. Count to 4 on the way down—resist gravity.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Use a rack to position the bar at knee height. Pull with your traps, not your lower back.
Pose.Name: Rack Pull
Pose.Equipment: Barbell with Heavy Plates, Power Rack with Safety Pins

steps.step: Set the safety pins in a power rack at knee height. Load the barbell with heavy plates. Stand over the bar with feet hip-width apart.
Grip the bar just outside your knees. Keep your back straight, chest up, and core braced.
Exhale as you pull the bar up, driving through your heels. Shrug your shoulders at the top.
Inhale as you lower the bar back to the pins with control. Perform 3 sets of 6 reps.
Rack pulls allow you to handle heavier weight than deadlifts, overloading the traps. Use straps if grip fails.

common_mistakes.mistake: Jerking the bar off the pins.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Pull the bar close to your body. Do not use momentum. Keep your shoulders down and back.
Pose.Name: Upright Row
Pose.Equipment: Barbell or EZ-Bar with Moderate Plates
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Shoulder impingement, rotator cuff injury, wrist tendinitis

steps.step: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly closer than shoulder-width. Let the bar hang at arm's length.
Exhale as you pull the bar straight up along your body, leading with your elbows. Keep the bar close to your torso.
Stop when the bar reaches upper chest level and your elbows are shoulder height.
Inhale as you lower with control. Perform 3 sets of 10 reps.
Do not pull the bar higher than your upper chest—this can impinge the shoulders. Use an EZ-bar for wrist comfort.

common_mistakes.mistake: Pulling the bar too high (past upper chest).
Using momentum to swing the bar.
Letting elbows drop below the bar.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Hinge at your hips. Do not round your lower back. Use a belt for heavy sets. This is a strength movement—form is critical.
Pose.Name: Barbell Bent-Over Row (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Heavy Barbell with Plates, Belt (Optional)
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Rhomboids, Trapezius (Middle), Latissimus Dorsi, Rear Deltoids, Biceps, Lower Back

steps.step: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent. Hinge at your hips until your torso is at 45 degrees. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Exhale as you pull the barbell toward your lower chest explosively. Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top.
Pause for 1 second at the peak.
Inhale as you lower with control over 2 seconds. Perform 5 sets of 5 reps.
At expert level, bent-over rows are a power movement. Use a belt, keep your back rigid, and don't sacrifice form for weight.

common_mistakes.mistake: Pulling with arms instead of back.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Start each rep from a dead stop. Do not bounce the weight. Squeeze your shoulder blades at the top.
Pose.Name: Pendlay Row
Pose.Equipment: Barbell with Heavy Plates
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Rhomboids, Trapezius, Latissimus Dorsi, Rear Deltoids, Biceps, Lower Back

steps.step: Stand over a barbell with feet shoulder-width apart. Hinge at your hips until your torso is parallel to the floor. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Exhale as you explosively pull the barbell toward your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades.
Pause briefly at the top.
Inhale as you lower the bar to the floor, coming to a dead stop. Perform 4 sets of 5 reps.
Pendlay rows build explosive back strength. Each rep starts from a dead stop—no touch-and-go. Use a belt for heavy sets.

common_mistakes.mistake: Bouncing the bar off the floor.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Use a chest pad for support. Squeeze your shoulder blades at the peak. Control the weight.
Pose.Name: T-Bar Row
Pose.Equipment: T-Bar Row Machine or Landmine Attachment with V-Handle
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Rhomboids, Trapezius (Middle), Latissimus Dorsi, Rear Deltoids, Biceps

steps.step: Position yourself on a T-bar row machine with your chest against the pad. Grip the handles with palms facing each other.
Exhale as you pull the handles toward your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for 2 seconds at the peak.
Inhale as you lower with control. Perform 4 sets of 8 reps.
T-bar rows are excellent for building a thick upper back. Keep your chest pressed against the pad to isolate the back muscles.

common_mistakes.mistake: Pulling with arms instead of back.
Not fully extending at the bottom.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Use a landmine attachment. Squeeze your lat at the peak. Control the movement throughout.
Pose.Name: Meadows Row
Pose.Equipment: Barbell, Landmine Attachment, Handle Attachment
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Latissimus Dorsi (Unilateral), Rhomboids, Trapezius, Rear Deltoids, Biceps

steps.step: Load one end of a barbell into a landmine attachment. Grip the loaded end with one hand. Stand with feet staggered, knees slightly bent.
Exhale as you pull the handle toward your hip, squeezing your lat. Keep your elbow close to your body.
Pause for 2 seconds at the peak.
Inhale as you lower with control. Perform 4 sets of 10 reps per side.
Meadows rows allow a heavy, unilateral row with a natural arc. They're excellent for building lat thickness and correcting imbalances.

common_mistakes.mistake: Pulling with arms instead of back.

Pose.safety: This is a drop set. Do not rest between drops. Control the weight throughout. Keep your back straight and chest up. Stop if form breaks.
Pose.Name: Seated Cable Row (Drop Set)
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with V-Grip, Multiple Weight Increments
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Rhomboids, Trapezius, Latissimus Dorsi, Rear Deltoids, Biceps

steps.step: Set up three weight increments on the cable row: heavy, medium, light.
Perform 12 reps with heavy weight. Squeeze your shoulder blades at the peak of each rep.
Immediately reduce weight to medium. Perform 8 more reps without rest.
Immediately reduce to light weight. Perform 6 more reps. Perform 3 drop sets total.
Drop sets on cable rows are intense. Perform them at the end of your back workout for a finishing pump.

common_mistakes.mistake: Pulling with arms instead of back.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Focus on external rotation of the shoulders. Use heavy weight but maintain form—this is a prehab and strength exercise.
Pose.Name: Face Pull (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with Rope Attachment
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Rear Deltoids, Trapezius (Middle/Lower), Rhomboids, Rotator Cuff

steps.step: Attach a rope to a high pulley. Stand facing the cable, holding the rope with an overhand grip. Step back to create tension.
Exhale as you pull the rope toward your face, separating the ends. Keep your elbows high (shoulder height).
At the peak, externally rotate your shoulders by pulling the rope ends past your ears. Squeeze your shoulder blades.
Inhale as you return with control. Perform 4 sets of 15 reps.
Even with heavy weight, the external rotation is key. If you can't rotate fully, the weight is too heavy.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using too much weight, losing rotation.
Not externally rotating at the peak.

Pose.safety: Use a controlled motion—no kipping. Squeeze your lats at the top. Keep your core braced. Use a belt for heavy weighted pull-ups if needed.
Pose.Name: Weighted Pull-Up (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Pull-Up Bar, Weight Belt, Heavy Plates
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Latissimus Dorsi (Primary), Biceps, Rhomboids, Trapezius, Core
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Shoulder impingement, elbow tendinitis, rotator cuff tear

steps.step: Attach heavy weight to a dip belt. Grip the pull-up bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulders.
Exhale as you pull yourself up until your chin clears the bar. Squeeze your lats at the top.
Pause for 1 second at the top.
Inhale as you slowly lower with control. Perform 5 sets of 5 reps.
At expert level, weighted pull-ups are a primary strength movement. Use a belt, keep strict form, and don't kip.

common_mistakes.mistake: Not fully extending arms at the bottom.

Pose.safety: Use a controlled motion—no kipping. Pull your chest to the bar for full range of motion. Squeeze your lats at the top.
Pose.Name: Chest-to-Bar Pull-Up
Pose.Equipment: Pull-Up Bar
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Latissimus Dorsi (Primary), Biceps, Rhomboids, Trapezius, Core

steps.step: Grip the pull-up bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulders.
Exhale as you pull yourself up explosively, aiming to touch your upper chest to the bar.
Pause for 1 second at the top, squeezing your lats.
Inhale as you slowly lower with control. Perform 4 sets of 8 reps.
Chest-to-bar pull-ups require more lat and upper back strength than chin-over-bar. If you can't reach chest, work on explosive pulling power.

Pose.safety: This is a drop set. Do not rest between drops. Control the weight throughout. Keep your back straight and chest up. Stop if form breaks.
Pose.Name: Lat Pulldown (Drop Set)
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with Wide Bar, Multiple Weight Increments

steps.step: Set up three weight increments on the lat pulldown: heavy, medium, light.
Perform 12 reps with heavy weight. Squeeze your lats at the bottom of each rep.
Immediately reduce weight to medium. Perform 8 more reps without rest.
Immediately reduce to light weight. Perform 6 more reps. Perform 3 drop sets total.
Drop sets on lat pulldowns create an incredible pump. Keep your form strict even as fatigue sets in.

common_mistakes.mistake: Letting the bar pull your arms up.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Squeeze your lat at the peak. Control the slow eccentric phase. Do not use momentum.
Pose.Name: Single-Arm Cable Row (Slow Eccentric)
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with Single Handle

steps.step: Attach a single handle to a low pulley. Sit at the cable row, gripping the handle with one hand. Place your other hand on your knee.
Exhale as you pull the handle toward your hip over 1 second. Squeeze your lat at the peak.
Pause for 2 seconds at the peak.
Inhale as you slowly return over 4 seconds. Perform 4 sets of 10 reps per side.
The 4-second eccentric builds lat size and strength. Fight the cable on the way back—don't let it pull you.

common_mistakes.mistake: Pulling with arms instead of lats.
Letting the weight stack touch between reps.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Squeeze your lats at the bottom. Keep your arms straight throughout. Use heavy weight with control.
Pose.Name: Straight Arm Pulldown (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Cable Machine with Rope or Straight Bar

steps.step: Attach a rope or straight bar to a high pulley. Stand facing the cable, holding the attachment with an overhand grip. Keep a slight bend in your elbows.
Exhale as you pull the attachment down toward your thighs, keeping your arms straight. Squeeze your lats at the bottom.
Pause for 2 seconds at the bottom.
Inhale as you slowly return. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
Even with heavy weight, keep your arms straight. If you find yourself bending your elbows, reduce the weight.

Pose.safety: This is a heavy strength movement. Use a belt. Keep your back straight—do not round at any point. Drive through your heels. Use a spotter or safety bars if needed.
Pose.Name: Conventional Deadlift (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Heavy Barbell with Plates, Deadlift Platform, Belt, Chalk
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Erector Spinae, Glutes, Hamstrings, Quadriceps, Traps, Forearms
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Lower back herniation, sciatica, hamstring tear

steps.step: Stand with feet hip-width apart, shins close to the bar. Grip the bar just outside your knees. Set your back flat, chest up, core braced.
Inhale and brace. Drive through your heels to stand up, keeping the bar close to your body.
At the top, squeeze your glutes and stand tall. Do not hyperextend.
Inhale as you lower the bar with control. Perform 5 sets of 3-5 reps.
At expert level, deadlifts are a test of strength. Use chalk, a belt, and proper bracing. Never sacrifice form for weight.

common_mistakes.mistake: Jerking the bar off the floor.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Use a wide stance. Drive through your heels. Do not round your back at any point.
Pose.Name: Sumo Deadlift (Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Barbell with Heavy Plates, Deadlift Platform, Belt
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Erector Spinae, Glutes, Adductors (Inner Thighs), Hamstrings, Quadriceps
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Lower back herniation, groin pull, knee injury

steps.step: Stand with feet wide (outside shoulder width), toes pointed outward. Grip the bar inside your knees, arms straight.
Inhale and brace your core. Drive through your heels to stand up, keeping your back straight. Push your knees outward.
At the top, squeeze your glutes and stand tall.
Inhale as you lower with control. Perform 4 sets of 5 reps.
Sumo deadlifts shift emphasis to the glutes and adductors. Keep your chest up and knees pushed outward.

common_mistakes.mistake: Pulling with arms instead of legs.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Hinge at your hips. Do not round your back. Use a belt for heavy sets.
Pose.Name: Romanian Deadlift (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Heavy Barbell with Plates, Belt

steps.step: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding a heavy barbell in front of your thighs. Keep a slight bend in your knees.
Inhale as you push your hips back, lowering the bar down your shins. Keep your back completely straight.
Lower until you feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings (typically mid-shin).
Exhale as you drive your hips forward to return to standing. Perform 4 sets of 6 reps.
At expert weight, the RDL is a demanding hamstring exercise. Use a belt and focus on the stretch—don't bounce.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Hinge at your hips. Do not round your back. Start with light weight to master form—this is unforgiving.
Pose.Name: Barbell Good Morning (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Barbell with Heavy Plates, Belt

steps.step: Place a barbell across your upper back (low bar position). Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent. Brace your core.
Inhale as you push your hips back, bending forward at the waist. Keep your back completely straight.
Lower your torso until it is parallel to the floor (or as far as flexibility allows).
Exhale as you drive your hips forward to return to standing. Perform 4 sets of 8 reps.
Good mornings are highly effective but unforgiving. Use a belt, video yourself, and stop if you feel any back rounding.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Move through a controlled range of motion. Squeeze your glutes and lower back at the top.
Pose.Name: Reverse Hyperextension (Weighted - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Reverse Hyperextension Machine or Bench with Weight Plate

steps.step: Position yourself face down on a reverse hyper machine with your hips on the pad. Attach weight to the foot holder.
Exhale as you lift your legs toward the ceiling, squeezing your glutes and lower back.
Pause for 2 seconds at the top.
Inhale as you lower with control. Perform 3 sets of 12 reps.
Reverse hypers decompress the spine while building lower back strength. Use a controlled tempo—no swinging.

common_mistakes.mistake: Letting hips lift off the pad.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Squeeze your traps at the top. Lift straight up and down—do not roll your shoulders. Use straps if needed.
Pose.Name: Barbell Shrug (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Heavy Barbell with Plates, Lifting Straps (Optional)

steps.step: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a heavy barbell in front of your thighs. Use straps if grip is limiting. Keep your back straight and core braced.
Exhale as you lift your shoulders straight up toward your ears as high as possible. Squeeze your traps at the top.
Pause for 2-3 seconds at the top.
Inhale as you slowly lower your shoulders back down. Perform 5 sets of 8 reps.
At expert level, shrugs are a heavy strength movement. Use straps to bypass grip limitations and focus on trap contraction.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using momentum to jerk the bar up.

Pose.safety: Keep your back straight and core braced. Use a rack to position the bar at knee height. Pull with your traps, not your lower back. Use straps.
Pose.Name: Rack Pull (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Barbell with Heavy Plates, Power Rack, Lifting Straps

steps.step: Set the safety pins in a power rack at knee height. Load the barbell with heavy plates. Stand over the bar with feet hip-width apart.
Grip the bar just outside your knees using straps. Keep your back straight, chest up, core braced.
Exhale as you pull the bar up, driving through your heels. Shrug your shoulders as hard as possible at the top.
Inhale as you lower the bar back to the pins with control. Perform 4 sets of 5 reps.
Rack pulls allow you to handle massive weight, overloading the traps. Use straps, a belt, and keep your back rigid.

common_mistakes.mistake: Jerking the bar off the pins.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Squeeze your traps at the top. Control the slow eccentric phase. Do not roll your shoulders.
Pose.Name: Dumbbell Shrug (Slow Eccentric - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Heavy Dumbbells

steps.step: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a heavy dumbbell in each hand at your sides. Keep your back straight and core braced.
Exhale as you lift your shoulders straight up toward your ears over 1 second. Squeeze your traps at the top.
Pause for 2 seconds at the top.
Inhale as you slowly lower your shoulders back down over 4 seconds. Perform 4 sets of 12 reps.
The slow eccentric (4-second descent) builds trap size. Use heavy dumbbells and count the negative—resist gravity.

Pose.safety: Keep your core braced and back straight. Pull the bar close to your body. Do not pull higher than upper chest. Use an EZ-bar for wrist comfort.
Pose.Name: Upright Row (Barbell - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Barbell or EZ-Bar with Moderate Plates

steps.step: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly closer than shoulder-width. Let the bar hang at arm's length.
Exhale as you pull the bar straight up along your body, leading with your elbows. Keep the bar close to your torso.
Stop when the bar reaches upper chest level and your elbows are shoulder height.
Inhale as you lower with control. Perform 3 sets of 10 reps.
Do not pull the bar higher than your upper chest—this impinges the shoulders. Use a wider grip if you have shoulder issues.

common_mistakes.mistake: Letting elbows drop below the bar.

Pose.safety: Keep your shoulders down and back. Maintain a straight posture. Walk with control—do not rush. Use heavy weight but maintain form.
Pose.Name: Farmer's Carry (Heavy - Expert)
Pose.Equipment: Heavy Dumbbells, Kettlebells, or Farmer's Walk Handles
Pose.Focused_Muscles: Forearm Flexors (Grip), Trapezius, Core Stabilizers, Glutes, Quadriceps
Pose.Not_For_Injuries: Lower back herniation, shoulder injury, grip tendinitis

steps.step: Pick up heavy dumbbells or farmer's handles from the floor with a flat back. Stand tall, shoulders back, core braced.
Walk forward with controlled, deliberate steps. Keep your chest up and gaze forward.
Do not let the weights swing. Keep your core braced and shoulders down.
Walk for 40 seconds, rest, then repeat. Perform 3 sets of 40 seconds.
At expert level, farmer's carries are a test of grip and full-body tension. Use heavy weight, walk tall, and don't let your shoulders shrug.

common_mistakes.mistake: Rounding the lower back when picking up.
